codegen.//: Baseline only. Every codegen target declares its own scoped inputs/outputs in project.json (project-level inputs REPLACE this default, they do not merge). Keep this default free of cross-boundary globs so a future un-scoped codegen target does not silently depend on unrelated boundaries.

conformance.//: Baseline only. Each conformance runner declares scoped inputs in project.json; project-level inputs REPLACE this default (Nx does not merge). Implementation-under-test coverage differs by language: TypeScript runners reach it through the Nx project graph via transitive ^production (kernel runners import the impl in-process; providers reach it via framework-runtime; framework runners spawn a built adapter subprocess with no import edge, so they declare an implicitDependencies edge to the adapter project to pull framework-runtime back into ^production). Rust runners instead need explicit implementations/rust/** globs because cargo crates are not modeled in the Nx project graph. Do not add cross-boundary or root tests/** globs here; that re-introduces cross-boundary cache poisoning. Known limitation: packet-driven plan SELECTION (boundaries/*/contracts/**/spec/authority-packet.json) is enforced by the authority-packet validation gate but is not part of the conformance cache key, so a rare repoint/removal of an existing plan selection that touches no conformance/** file can serve a stale cached result.
